activities_projects.title: Average Speed Calculation
activities_projects.objective: Understand and calculate average speed over multiple trials
steps: Measure a fixed distance
Time multiple trials of the moving object
Record all times in notebook
Calculate average speed using total distance/total time
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies average speed formula and analyzes repeated measurements

activities_projects.title: Comparing Uniform and Non-uniform Motion
activities_projects.objective: Analyze motion type by measuring distance and time intervals
steps: Mark equal segments on a track
Time the object crossing each segment
Calculate speed for each segment
Determine whether motion is uniform or non-uniform
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between uniform and non-uniform motion quantitatively

activities_projects.title: Plotting Distance-Time Graph
activities_projects.objective: Visualize motion and interpret speed from slope
materials: Data from toy car or walking experiment
steps: Record distance and time data from experiment
Plot distance on Y-axis and time on X-axis
Analyze slope to determine speed
Identify uniform or non-uniform motion from graph

activities_projects.title: Pendulum Time Period Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Study factors affecting time period of a pendulum
steps: Set up a simple pendulum
Measure time for 10 swings
Change length of pendulum and repeat
Analyze how length affects time period
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates pendulum length to time period quantitatively

activities_projects.title: Reaction Time Experiment – Multiple Trials
activities_projects.objective: Measure human reaction time and analyze variations
steps: Hold ruler between thumb and finger
Partner releases ruler randomly several times
Record distances for each trial
Calculate reaction time for each and average
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands human reaction time and variability

activities_projects.title: Comparing Motion on Different Surfaces
activities_projects.objective: Observe effect of surface type on speed
steps: Set equal track length on different surfaces
Time toy car moving on each surface
Calculate speed and compare
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates friction to motion and speed

activities_projects.title: Time Measurement Using Different Devices
activities_projects.objective: Compare accuracy of digital stopwatch, analog stopwatch, and mobile timer
steps: Measure same activity using all three devices
Record times
Compare precision and discuss differences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands precision and reliability of different time-measuring devices

activities_projects.title: Calculating Instantaneous Speed
activities_projects.objective: Estimate instantaneous speed using short intervals
steps: Mark small intervals along track
Record time taken for each interval
Calculate speed for each interval
Compare with average speed

activities_projects.title: Effect of Mass on Speed
activities_projects.objective: Study how object’s mass affects motion down a slope
steps: Set up ramp and measure slope length
Change weight of toy car
Record time to reach bottom for each weight
Calculate speed and analyze effect of mass
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes effect of mass on speed quantitatively
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate measurement, calculation, and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Design Your Own Motion Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Apply concepts creatively in a self-designed motion study
materials: Objects of choice (toy car, ball, bicycle)
steps: Design an experiment involving motion and time measurement
Predict expected results
Perform experiment and record observations
Compare results with predictions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Encourages creativity, planning, and analytical skills in motion study
